2605.02392.title: Is It Novel and Why? Fine-Grained Patent Novelty Prediction Based on Passage Retrieval
2605.02392.paper_summary: Novelty assessment is a critical yet complex task in the examination process for patent acceptance, requiring examiners to determine whether an invention is disclosed in a prior art document. The process involves intricate matching between specific features of a patent claim and passages in the prior art. While prior work has approached novelty prediction primarily as a binary classification task at the claim level, we argue that this formulation is susceptible to spurious correlations and lacks the granularity required for practical application. In this work, we introduce FiNE-Patents (Fine-grained Novelty Examination of Patents), a novel dataset comprising 3,658 first patent claims annotated with fine-grained, feature-level prior art references extracted from European Search Opinion (ESOP) documents. We propose shifting the evaluation paradigm from simple binary classification to a joint retrieval and abstract reasoning task at the feature level, requiring models to identify specific passages from a prior art document that disclose individual claim features, and to identify which features of a claim make it novel. We implement and evaluate LLM-based workflows that decompose claims into features, analyze each feature against prior art, and finally derive a claim-level novelty prediction. Our experiments demonstrate that these workflows outperform embedding-based baselines on passage retrieval and novel feature identification. Furthermore, we show that unlike trained classifiers, LLMs are robust against spurious correlations present in the claim-level novelty classification task. We release the dataset and code to foster further research into transparent and granular patent analysis.
2605.02392.paper_summary_zh: 新穎性評估是在專利接受審查過程中一項關鍵但複雜的任務，要求審查員確定某項發明是否在先前的技術文件中披露。這一過程涉及專利請求的特定特徵與先前技術中的段落之間的複雜匹配。雖然先前的工作主要將新穎性預測視為一項二元分類任務，但我們認為這種表述容易受到虛假相關的影響，並且缺乏實際應用所需的細緻度。在這項工作中，我們介紹了FiNE-Patents（專利的新穎性細粒度檢查），這是一個新穎的數據集，包含3,658個第一專利請求，並附有從歐洲檢索意見（ESOP）文件中提取的細粒度、特徵級的先前技術參考。我們建議將評估範式從簡單的二元分類轉變為特徵級的聯合檢索和抽象推理任務，要求模型識別先前技術文件中披露個別請求特徵的具體段落，並確定哪些請求特徵使其具有新穎性。我們實施並評估基於LLM的工作流程，將請求分解為特徵，分析每個特徵與先前技術的關係，並最終得出請求級的新穎性預測。我們的實驗表明，這些工作流程在段落檢索和新穎特徵識別方面的表現超過了基於嵌入的基準。此外，我們顯示，與訓練的分類器不同，LLM對請求級新穎性分類任務中存在的虛假相關具有穩健性。我們發布了數據集和代碼，以促進對透明和細粒度專利分析的進一步研究。
2605.02392.author: Valentin Knappich et.al.
2605.02392.authors: Valentin Knappich, Anna Hätty, Simon Razniewski, Annemarie Friedrich
